requirement: Touchscreen input
evidence_summary: User asks for recommendations for laptops for online college courses and prefers touch screen.

requirement: Reputable purchase or financing options
evidence_summary: User considering a rent‑to‑own site for a laptop and is concerned about hidden charges and data security.

requirement: Guidance on laptop specifications for engineering studies

requirement: Operating system forward‑compatibility (Windows 12 readiness)
evidence_summary: User wants a school laptop that is compatible with future Windows 12 and not limited to Windows 11.

requirement: Low purchase price (≈100 EUR used segment)
evidence_summary: User asks for older used laptops around 100 euro for university/programming.

requirement: Thin and non‑bulky chassis
evidence_summary: User wants a used university/programming laptop that is not bulky.

requirement: High multitasking performance (many browser tabs and apps)
evidence_summary: User with ADHD keeps many tabs open and needs a laptop that can handle that realistically.

requirement: Compatibility with required academic software

requirement: OS familiarity and low learning curve

requirement: Seamless ecosystem integration (Apple device sync)

requirement: Good support for Microsoft Office and Teams

requirement: Lightweight body for daily campus carry

requirement: Long battery life for full day of classes
evidence_summary: Multiple comments emphasize needing battery that lasts through school days (10+ hours mentioned).

requirement: Compact power adapter
evidence_summary: User complains about a huge gaming‑laptop brick charger adding weight to their bag.

requirement: Adequate RAM capacity (minimum 16 GB for students)
evidence_summary: Several users recommend at least 16 GB RAM for engineering, coding, multitasking, and future‑proofing.

requirement: Upgradeable RAM

requirement: Large internal storage capacity (≥1 TB)

requirement: High‑performance CPU for engineering and CAD
evidence_summary: Engineering and CAD users request decently high‑powered laptops and mention powerful Ryzen/Intel chips.

requirement: Discrete GPU not mandatory for most engineering students

requirement: Integrated GPU support for CAD and engineering tools

requirement: Screen size around 15.5–16 inches for productivity
evidence_summary: User recommends 16" screen for extra real estate and would not go below 15.5–16".

requirement: Small screen size (≤14 inches) for portability
evidence_summary: Another user explicitly wants a screen of 14 inches or less and low weight for grad classes and work.

requirement: Quiet fan operation
evidence_summary: Student wants a lightweight school laptop with quiet fans.

requirement: Durable chassis and hinges
evidence_summary: Multiple users mention durability concerns, including hinges breaking and desire for laptops that last years of school use.

requirement: Minimum service life of 4–7 years

requirement: Good brand reliability and build quality

requirement: Educational or student discounts
evidence_summary: Users mention MacBook education discounts and student discounts from manufacturers like Apple and Lenovo.

requirement: Institutional support and repair contracts
evidence_summary: Advice to check what brands the school services or has repair contracts for, to ensure warranty support.

requirement: Magnetic power connector or safe charging port

requirement: Good Wi‑Fi performance and stability

requirement: Chromebook suitability for basic school tasks

requirement: Long‑term software support period
evidence_summary: Chromebook support up to 10 years and concerns about Windows 10/11 end of support are mentioned.

requirement: Linux compatibility for extending life of old laptops

requirement: Good battery life under light academic workloads

requirement: High‑quality display for reading and note‑taking

requirement: Avoidance of OLED panels due to burn‑in risk

requirement: Pen or stylus input support

requirement: Comfortable tablet mode for long note‑taking sessions

requirement: High‑quality stylus technology and palm rejection

requirement: 2‑in‑1 hinge durability

requirement: Separate tablet plus standard laptop option

requirement: Low device weight for commuting students
evidence_summary: Students with long commutes emphasize ultra‑light laptops to reduce bag weight.

requirement: Good thermal management and cool operation
evidence_summary: Chromebooks and some laptops are praised for running cooler; others mention hot gaming laptops.

requirement: Quiet operation in classrooms
evidence_summary: User does not want a laptop that sounds like an airplane in class.

requirement: Adequate performance for office and browser‑based workloads
evidence_summary: Many comments state that for documents, email, and web, modest hardware is sufficient.

requirement: Support for common academic tools (Office, browsers, e‑learning)
evidence_summary: Users mention Office 365, Google Docs, e‑learning platforms, and browser‑based coursework.

requirement: Support for STEM and engineering software (e.g., CAD, Solidworks, Vivado, Keil)
evidence_summary: Engineering and CE students reference Solidworks, AutoCAD, Vivado, Keil, and lab equipment software.

requirement: Windows OS for business, accounting, and some STEM programs

requirement: Avoidance of Apple Silicon for some CE toolchains

requirement: Support for remote desktop or virtualized campus services

requirement: Good keyboard quality for long typing sessions

requirement: Backlit keyboard option

requirement: Adequate port selection for external displays and peripherals
evidence_summary: Users discuss HDMI, mini‑DP, USB‑C display out, and note full‑size DisplayPort is rare on student laptops.

requirement: Support for external monitor use
evidence_summary: Several users describe using external monitors for school work and productivity.

requirement: Good battery capacity (≥50 Wh for 14", ≥70 Wh for 15–16")
evidence_summary: One user explicitly recommends these battery sizes for students depending on laptop size.

requirement: Minimum 8 GB RAM for basic student workloads
evidence_summary: Examples of 8 GB M1 Air and 8 GB Windows laptops being sufficient for standard school tasks.

requirement: Preference for 16 GB RAM for productivity and future‑proofing
evidence_summary: Multiple users say 16 GB is ideal for coding, many tabs, and long‑term use.

requirement: SSD storage instead of HDD
evidence_summary: Users discuss NVMe SSDs, upgrading SSDs, and avoiding slow hard drives for school laptops.

requirement: Easy storage upgradeability
evidence_summary: Several users mention installing larger SSDs (e.g., 2 TB) in school laptops.

requirement: Robust hinge design for frequent opening/closing

requirement: Good value in refurbished business‑class laptops
evidence_summary: Multiple users recommend refurbished ThinkPads, Latitudes, and Yogas as durable, affordable student options.

requirement: Reasonable device weight even with accessories
evidence_summary: Students describe carrying laptop, stand, mouse, keyboard, and charger around campus.

requirement: Support for note‑taking apps (OneNote, etc.)
evidence_summary: User mentions Surface Laptop with OneNote for drawing on slides during class.

requirement: Good battery life for note‑taking tablets
evidence_summary: Users praise iPads and Tab S11 Ultra for note‑taking with long battery and AppleCare support.

requirement: Low system resource usage for basic academic tasks
evidence_summary: Users note that even low‑end Celeron/4 GB RAM machines can handle basic note‑taking and Office.

requirement: Support for coding and development tools
evidence_summary: Students mention learning Python, C#, cyber security, and needing laptops that handle coding environments.

requirement: Support for remote servers and SSH workflows
evidence_summary: User mainly works on remote servers and uses local laptop for coding, research, and note‑taking.

requirement: Stable OS and driver behavior for sleep/suspend
evidence_summary: User criticizes Linux laptop suspend behavior and values stability for mobile academic use.

requirement: Good thermal design for cool, quiet classroom use
evidence_summary: Students compare cool Chromebooks and cool‑running ultrabooks vs hot gaming laptops.

requirement: Non‑distracting device setup for focus
evidence_summary: User argues that extra gadgets like tablets and smartwatches can be distractions in college.

requirement: Clear manufacturer battery and power specs
evidence_summary: User notes Lenovo does not state battery wattage and wants the one with larger battery as a student.

requirement: Resilience to accidental spills with proper shutdown
evidence_summary: Discussion on water damage emphasizes powering off and drying to avoid short circuits.

requirement: Institutional policy compatibility (MDM/Intune removability)
evidence_summary: Users discuss school‑locked laptops, Intune admin portals, and ways to repurpose such devices.

requirement: Adequate performance for online cyber security coursework
evidence_summary: Cyber student notes that any laptop is fine for watching videos, reading e‑books, and using 365 products.

requirement: Support for external storage for large project files
evidence_summary: User plans to put school programs on an external drive for CAD and similar tasks.

requirement: Good price‑to‑performance ratio for student budgets
evidence_summary: Many users emphasize affordability, refurbished options, and best bang for buck for students.

requirement: Strong battery life on ARM/Snapdragon laptops for light workloads
evidence_summary: User notes Snapdragon laptops are fine for browser and office‑suite‑based school work.

requirement: Reliable performance for simultaneous apps and many tabs
evidence_summary: User often runs ~10 apps and 20 Chrome tabs for coding, research, and note‑taking.

requirement: Non‑Mac Windows laptop preference for some users
evidence_summary: User explicitly wants Windows and not a MacBook because they do not use Apple products.

requirement: Good on‑campus repair and support turnaround
evidence_summary: Apple Stores near campus are praised for quick repairs vs shipping Windows laptops to service centers.

requirement: Sufficient performance for light gaming and emulation if needed
evidence_summary: Students mention iGPUs rivaling older GTX 1050 and running favorite games at that level.

requirement: Avoidance of midrange gaming laptops for school use
evidence_summary: User calls midrange gaming laptops awful for school due to weight, durability, and poor battery life.

requirement: Clear manufacturer communication of OS support lifecycle
evidence_summary: Concerns about Windows 10/11 end of support and Chromebook support timelines.

requirement: Support for external lab equipment connectivity
evidence_summary: Student mentions needing Windows PC to connect to lab software for blood analyzers and microscopes.

requirement: Adequate performance for data analysis plugins and databases
evidence_summary: Student runs Excel with data analytics plugins and Access databases all day on a single charge.

requirement: Good battery life under heavy academic multitasking
evidence_summary: Student uses laptop from 10am–10pm with multiple Office apps and browser tabs and still has battery left.

requirement: Support for external GPU not required for typical coursework
evidence_summary: Several comments imply that for most academic tasks, integrated graphics are sufficient.

requirement: Reasonable device thermals for lap use in lectures
evidence_summary: MacBook Airs are praised for never outputting heat and being ideal for student use.

requirement: High efficiency and long battery life (Apple Silicon class)
evidence_summary: M1/M2/M4 MacBook Airs are repeatedly cited as extremely efficient with very long battery life for students.

requirement: Sufficient performance for AI coursework via cloud services
evidence_summary: User notes AI work is done on servers/Colab and that a normal i5 with 16 GB RAM is enough locally.

requirement: Ability to run multiple heavy engineering programs if needed
evidence_summary: Asus Zephyrus G14 and similar laptops are popular among students who run heavy software.

requirement: Good screen readability for long study sessions
evidence_summary: Users mention screen quality and color accuracy affecting comfort and desire to use the laptop.

requirement: Support for external numeric keypad or built‑in numpad
evidence_summary: Some laptops are praised for having full numpads, useful for accounting/engineering work.

requirement: Reasonable fan noise under typical academic loads
evidence_summary: Students want laptops that remain quiet during lectures and library use.

requirement: Dedicated gaming GPU (RTX 3050 or better)
evidence_summary: Users insist that a gaming laptop needs a strong GPU and recommend at least RTX 3050/3050 6GB, 4050, 4060, 5050, or 5060 for AAA titles and future‑proofing.

requirement: Entry-level gaming GPU (RTX 3050 / 4050 / 5050 class) for 1080p gaming
evidence_summary: Multiple comments say RTX 3050/4050/5050 are fine for 1080p high settings and 60 fps in most games if settings are tuned.

requirement: Mid-range gaming GPU (RTX 4060 / 5060) for AAA titles
evidence_summary: Users recommend 4060/5060 laptops for AAA games like GTA VI and newer demanding titles instead of weaker GPUs.

requirement: High-end gaming GPU (RTX 4070 / 5070 / 5070 Ti) option
evidence_summary: Some users with higher budgets want RTX 4070/5070/5070 Ti for 2K gaming and higher VRAM for future titles.

requirement: GPU power limit (high TGP, e.g., 130W+ where chassis allows)
evidence_summary: Users compare 3060/3070/4070 variants by wattage and note that higher TGP versions perform much better in games.

requirement: VRAM capacity (minimum 6 GB, 8 GB preferred)
evidence_summary: Comments say 3050 6GB can handle most games on high/ultra, and 8GB VRAM on 4050/4060/4070 is desirable for modern titles.

requirement: Integrated GPU gaming capability (e.g., 780M / 860M / 130V / 140V / 140T)
evidence_summary: Several users highlight Ryzen 7 780M, Ryzen AI 7 350 (860M), and Intel 130V/140V/140T iGPUs as able to run light and some AAA games at low‑medium settings.

requirement: Support for modern upscaling (DLSS / FSR / XeSS)
evidence_summary: Gamers mention DLSS and FSR as game‑changers for performance and note that older iGPUs like Vega 8 lack FSR support.

requirement: Gaming driver optimization and update cadence
evidence_summary: Users distinguish between game‑ready drivers and generic ones, and complain about newer drivers breaking 30/40‑series cards.

requirement: CPU performance (modern 6–8 performance cores or equivalent)
evidence_summary: Gamers compare i5/i7 13th gen, Ultra 5/7, Ryzen 5/7/9 and say older low‑power U‑series and Atom CPUs are not suitable for modern games.

requirement: CPU efficiency (low power, cool operation for non-gaming use)
evidence_summary: Users praise Ultra 5 225H/226V and Ryzen AI chips for running cooler, quieter, and giving better battery life than older H/HX CPUs.

requirement: CPU upgradability (desktop or socketed where possible)
evidence_summary: One user notes older gaming laptops where you could buy a cheaper GPU and upgrade later, contrasting with today’s soldered designs.

requirement: System RAM capacity (minimum 16 GB for gaming)
evidence_summary: Many comments say 8GB is underwhelming or minimum, and recommend at least 16GB for gaming and modern workloads.

requirement: System RAM capacity (32 GB for heavy gaming and multitasking)
evidence_summary: Users with 32GB+ report smoother gaming and recommend 32GB for AAA titles, ML, and future‑proofing.

requirement: RAM type (DDR5 or LPDDR5X preferred)
evidence_summary: Gamers call DDR4 “dated” and prefer DDR5/LPDDR5X for higher bandwidth, especially for iGPU performance.

requirement: Dual-channel memory configuration
evidence_summary: Several posts mention dual sticks (2x8, 2x16) and note that iGPU performance massively improves with dual‑channel RAM.

requirement: User-upgradable RAM (SO-DIMM slots, not fully soldered)
evidence_summary: Many users ask if they can upgrade 8GB to 16/32GB and complain about soldered RAM limiting longevity.

requirement: User-upgradable storage (at least one spare M.2 slot)
evidence_summary: Gamers describe adding second NVMe SSDs and value laptops with two M.2 slots for easy storage expansion.

requirement: Internal SSD capacity (minimum 512 GB)
evidence_summary: Multiple users say 256GB is not enough in 2026 and that 512GB is a practical minimum for OS plus a few games.

requirement: Internal SSD capacity (1 TB or more preferred for gaming)
evidence_summary: Gamers with large libraries mention multi‑TB SSDs and say many games can use multiple terabytes of storage.

requirement: NVMe SSD performance (PCIe 3.0 or better, low latency)
evidence_summary: Users list PCIe 4.0 NVMe drives and note HDDs make Windows and games feel super slow until replaced with SSDs.

requirement: External storage support (USB-C / USB-A for external SSDs)
evidence_summary: Several users keep games or backups on external SSDs and discuss performance and lag when running games externally.

requirement: Display refresh rate (minimum 60 Hz, 120–165 Hz preferred)
evidence_summary: Gamers mention 60Hz being fine for some, but 120/144/165/180/240Hz panels are praised for smoother gaming.

requirement: Display resolution (1080p or 1440p native for gaming)
evidence_summary: Most gaming setups are 1080p or 1440p; users note 3K/4K is too demanding for iGPUs and often require lowering resolution.

requirement: Display response time (low ms for gaming panels)
evidence_summary: Specs like 3ms response on 165Hz panels are highlighted as desirable for shooters and fast games.

requirement: Variable refresh / adaptive sync support (G-SYNC / FreeSync / VRR)
evidence_summary: Users mention NVIDIA G-SYNC, adaptive sync, and screen tearing issues when refresh rates don’t match and sync is off.

requirement: Panel type (IPS or OLED with good viewing angles)
evidence_summary: Gamers and creators praise IPS and OLED panels for color and viewing angles compared to older TN panels.

requirement: Color gamut coverage (sRGB 100% or better)
evidence_summary: Some users explicitly mention 100% sRGB and DCI-P3 coverage as desirable for both gaming and content work.

requirement: OLED display option
evidence_summary: Many posts praise OLED screens (2.8K/3K, 500–600 nits) for striking colors and HDR gaming and media.

requirement: HDR support on display
evidence_summary: Users mention enabling HDR in games on OLED TVs and laptops for significantly better visuals.

requirement: Matte or anti-glare screen option
evidence_summary: Gamers discuss matte vs glossy; matte reduces reflections but can make text fuzzy, glossy is sharp but reflective.

requirement: Screen size options (14–16 inch for gaming/portability balance)
evidence_summary: People debate 14" Zephyrus vs 16" Legion/IdeaPad Pro as sweet spots between portability and gaming immersion.

requirement: Thermal design for sustained gaming (no severe throttling)
evidence_summary: Users complain about thin laptops throttling and praise thicker Legions/Predators with better thermals for long sessions.

requirement: Safe sustained CPU temperature (≤85–90°C under load)
evidence_summary: Multiple comments say 80–90°C is normal for gaming laptops, but above ~95°C or constant throttling is concerning.

requirement: Safe sustained GPU temperature (≤80–85°C under load)
evidence_summary: Gamers report GPUs around 80°C during long sessions and consider that acceptable for longevity.

requirement: Fan noise level (reduced noise in non-gaming use)
evidence_summary: Users want balanced/performance modes only when gaming and quieter operation for everyday tasks.

requirement: Fan control software (user-adjustable fan curves)
evidence_summary: People ask about changing fan speed (Acer, Lenovo, Asus apps) and want manual control for cooling vs noise.

requirement: Support for cooling pads or stands (bottom intake clearance)
evidence_summary: Several recommend cooling pads/stands for gaming laptops and stress the need for good ventilation underneath.

requirement: Dust management and easy cleaning access
evidence_summary: Users mention blowing out dust, cleaning fans, and note that hinges and vents collect dirt and need maintenance.

requirement: Thermal paste quality and serviceability
evidence_summary: Some users talk about repasting CPUs/GPUs to reduce temps and complain about OEMs using poor or dried paste.

requirement: Charger power rating (sufficient wattage to avoid battery drain under load)
evidence_summary: One user worries about a 130W adapter not keeping up so the battery drains while gaming; others say gaming laptops need 170W+ or 230W+ bricks.

requirement: Battery capacity (70–99 Wh for gaming laptops)
evidence_summary: Specs like 75Wh, 80Wh, 84Wh, 90Wh, 99Wh are highlighted; larger batteries are valued for non‑gaming use.

requirement: Battery runtime (2–4 hours gaming, 8–12 hours light use)
evidence_summary: Users report 2–3 hours gaming vs 8–12 hours browsing/video on efficient laptops and accept short gaming runtime.

requirement: Battery charge limit control (e.g., 80% cap option)
evidence_summary: Several discuss limiting charge to 80% or 90–95% to reduce wear, especially when plugged in most of the time.

requirement: Battery charge threshold control (start/stop levels configurable)
evidence_summary: Users mention settings like “don’t start charging until 90–95%” to avoid unnecessary charge cycles.

requirement: Battery health monitoring and calibration support
evidence_summary: Comments reference calibrating runtime by fully charging occasionally and monitoring degradation over years.

requirement: Support for USB-C charging (Power Delivery)
evidence_summary: Some users mention 65W/120W USB‑C power banks and want to use smaller chargers for travel and light use.

requirement: Battery not required for full performance when plugged in
evidence_summary: Users dislike designs where the laptop draws from battery plus adapter for max performance, reducing battery life.

requirement: Chassis material quality (metal or reinforced plastic)
evidence_summary: People ask if laptops are metallic or plastic and praise all‑aluminum Omnibooks/Zenbooks over cheap plastic Vivobooks.

requirement: Hinge durability (no cracking or breaking over time)
evidence_summary: Multiple users mention broken hinges and say budget gaming laptops often have hinge issues after a few years.

requirement: Overall build quality and rigidity
evidence_summary: Legion, ThinkPad, ZBook, and ProArt lines are praised for solid build; gaming laptops are often criticized as flimsy.

requirement: Keyboard durability and key switch longevity
evidence_summary: Long‑term users of ROG/Legion note years of gaming; others mention failing keys on cheaper models.

requirement: Trackpad accuracy and comfort (centered where possible)
evidence_summary: One user dislikes an off‑center trackpad on a Flex 5i and prefers MacBook‑like large, accurate trackpads.

requirement: Keyboard backlighting (RGB or single-color)
evidence_summary: Gamers discuss RGB lighting reacting to media/notifications and wanting static profiles for consistency.

requirement: Speaker quality (volume and bass for media and games)
evidence_summary: Yoga 9i, Zephyrus G14, and some Omnibooks are praised for strong speakers comparable to desktop sets.

requirement: Webcam quality (1080p with IR or Windows Hello optional)
evidence_summary: Some laptops list IR scanners for facial recognition; users expect decent video call quality.

requirement: Port selection (USB-A, USB-C, HDMI, audio jack, Ethernet)
evidence_summary: Gamers complain about thin laptops needing hubs and want multiple USB‑A, USB‑C, HDMI, Ethernet, and 3.5mm jacks.

requirement: Thunderbolt or high-speed USB-C (for docks and eGPU)
evidence_summary: Users mention Thunderbolt for docking and potential eGPU use to turn a thin laptop into a gaming machine.

requirement: SD or microSD card reader
evidence_summary: Creators and hobbyists mention card readers for art, photography, and convenience.

requirement: Wi-Fi standard (Wi-Fi 6E or Wi-Fi 7 support)
evidence_summary: One user notes Wi‑Fi 7 as the newest common one and stresses matching router and laptop for fast downloads and low latency.

requirement: Network latency performance (low ping for online games)
evidence_summary: Gamers discuss delay vs bandwidth and emphasize low delay for competitive titles.

requirement: Ethernet port availability or dongle support
evidence_summary: Some insist gaming laptops should have Ethernet for stable online play, especially when Wi‑Fi is congested.

requirement: Weight limit (around 1.3–2.2 kg depending on use)
evidence_summary: Users compare 14" ~1.3kg ultrabooks vs 16" 2kg+ gaming laptops and note portability trade‑offs.

requirement: Chassis thickness (option for thicker, cooler gaming models)
evidence_summary: Several say they don’t care about thinness and prefer thicker laptops with better cooling and more ports.

requirement: Battery life in non-gaming use (8+ hours target)
evidence_summary: Students and workers want all‑day battery for browsing, coding, and media, contrasting with 2–4 hours on gaming rigs.

requirement: Operating system compatibility with games (Windows x86)
evidence_summary: Users warn that macOS and Windows on ARM have limited native game support and require translation layers.

requirement: Linux gaming compatibility (Proton / SteamOS support)
evidence_summary: Some mention Nobara, Bazzite, ProtonDB, and Steam Deck compatibility for running games on Linux.

requirement: Virtualization and dual-boot support
evidence_summary: Users describe dual‑boot setups (Win7/Win10) and using VMs for legacy games and experimentation.

requirement: Cloud gaming compatibility (GeForce Now / xCloud etc.)

requirement: AI/ML workload support (dGPU with CUDA or strong NPU)
evidence_summary: Some mention needing dGPUs for AI/ML and others highlight NPUs in Ultra/Ryzen AI chips for on‑device AI tasks.

requirement: Emulator performance (retro consoles and handhelds)

requirement: Game performance at 1080p 60 fps (medium-high settings)
evidence_summary: Many references to running AAA games at 1080p high/ultra 60 fps with midrange GPUs like 3060/4060/5050.

requirement: Game performance at 1440p (QHD) 60–120 fps
evidence_summary: Owners of 1440p 120–240Hz panels describe playing modern games at QHD with DLSS and tuned settings.

requirement: Esports game performance (high fps in Valorant / CS / Fortnite)

requirement: Light gaming capability (indie and older titles)

requirement: Modded Minecraft performance (with shaders and mods)

requirement: Game storage management (support for large libraries)
evidence_summary: Gamers with multi‑TB SSDs and external drives note that modern games can consume terabytes of space.

requirement: Thermal throttling protection (automatic downclocking before damage)
evidence_summary: Users note laptops will throttle before dangerous temps and describe feeling slowdowns when throttling kicks in.

requirement: Power profiles (performance / balanced / efficiency modes)

requirement: BIOS and firmware stability (no overheating bugs)
evidence_summary: Some mention specific BIOS versions and overheating/throttling behavior, implying need for stable firmware.

requirement: Long-term reliability (5+ years of usable performance)

requirement: Component repairability (non-soldered SSD and RAM, accessible internals)

requirement: Hinge and chassis serviceability (replaceable parts)
evidence_summary: Users mention repairing hinges and cases on older laptops and want parts that can be replaced instead of scrapping the device.

requirement: Price-to-performance ratio (value in midrange segment)

requirement: Used / refurbished market viability

requirement: Regional availability and pricing parity
evidence_summary: Users in India, Germany, Chile, UAE mention local pricing differences and limited availability affecting choices.

requirement: Aesthetics suitable for work and gaming (non-flashy design option)

requirement: Weight and size acceptable for commuting and classes
evidence_summary: Students mention carrying laptops all day, preferring lighter devices for uni and using desktops for heavy gaming.

requirement: Docking support for desktop-like setup (external monitor, keyboard, mouse)
evidence_summary: Many describe using gaming laptops docked to 27" monitors, keyboards, mice, and cooling pads at home.

requirement: Support for external GPU (Thunderbolt / OCuLink)

requirement: Noise and heat acceptable for lap use (lap mode or underclock option)

requirement: Operating system bloatware minimization

requirement: Ability to handle multiple browser windows and media simultaneously
evidence_summary: Users describe having 10 browser windows, podcasts, and YouTube open at once without issues.

requirement: Support for cloud services and web apps
evidence_summary: Users rely on O365, Photopea, and other browser‑based tools instead of heavy local apps.

requirement: No requirement for high‑end gaming performance
evidence_summary: Many explicitly say ‘no gaming’ or only very light/old games; gaming is offloaded to desktops or consoles.
